items.title: Greyhound racing ban kept alive as independent indicates support

children.content: Upper house independent Dean Harriss, who is up for re-election next month, says he will support the Tasmanian government's ban on greyhound racing, but only if the industry compensation package is increased.

gallery.altText: Serious Dean in grey suit, light tie sits in chambers, wooden paneling, benches, red upholstry.
Greyhound dogs racing on a track in Sydney.

thumbnail.altText: Serious Dean in grey suit, light tie sits in chambers, wooden paneling, benches, red upholstry.
Greyhound dogs racing on a track in Sydney.

items.title: Hobart to ditch hire e-scooters and bring in 'safer' e-bikes instead

children.content: The City of Hobart said e-bikes were a safer alternative to the e-scooters, which have continued to draw safety concerns from residents and visitors.

image.altText: Orange and purple e-scooters blocking a footpath

items.title: Group that helped find Celine Cremer plan their next missing person search

children.content: After helping find missing tourist Celine Cremer, some of the search volunteers plan to formalise their group and take on other missing persons cases, including that of a man who went missing last year.

image.altText: They both look at a map on a car bonnet.

items.title: Deal struck between Tasmanian and federal governments to 'unlock' 4,000 homes

children.content: A peak construction industry body has welcomed the announcement of a $165 million funding agreement between the state and federal government, to build thousands of homes in Tasmania.

gallery.altText: The wooden frame of a house on brown brick foundations on an overcast day

thumbnail.altText: The wooden frame of a house on brown brick foundations on an overcast day

items.title: Leave without pay, or lose your job — decision day for smelter workers

children.content: With Australian and international bidders apparently eyeing off the troubled Liberty Bell Bay smelter, the workers and their families just want to know they'll have a job at the end of it all.

image.altText: Images from rally of workers.

items.title: Giant trees planted pre-WWII being removed after chemicals, disease, vandalism take toll

children.content: Two iconic giant sequoias, planted to mark the coronation of King George VI and Queen Elizabeth, are being removed from a Hobart park from Monday after their health rapidly declined.

image.altText: Dead giant sequoia trees in a park.

items.title: Salmon mortality data shows 9,000 tonnes of dead fish in three months

children.content: Environment Protection Authority data shows 9,000 tonnes of salmon died in the first three months of this year, despite the use of an antibiotic to tackle fish disease.

gallery.altText: Dead pink fish floating in an open ocean fish pen.

thumbnail.altText: Dead pink fish floating in an open ocean fish pen.

items.title: What to know about new CEO of one of world's biggest tech companies

children.content: The new CEO of Apple will be tasked with catching up in the AI race and continuing the company's legacy as a dominating force in the technology world.

image.altText: John Ternus presents at a apple hardware unveiling on a stage

items.title: New Zealand prime minister survives confidence vote

children.content: New Zealand Prime Minister Christopher Luxon says he has the support of his party after calling and winning a vote of confidence in his leadership.

image.altText: A bald man in a blue suit with a light blue tie and a New Zealand fern leaf pin looks at the camera

items.title: Residents urged to evacuate New Zealand's capital after floods

children.content: People living in low-lying areas of New Zealand's capital city, Wellington, are urged to evacuate as heavy rain drenches the city.

gallery.altText: traffic lights and signage enveloped in floods.

thumbnail.altText: traffic lights and signage enveloped in floods.

items.title: Dezi Freeman's shipping container entered by YouTubers, police investigate

children.content: YouTubers are being investigated for trespass after filming inside the converted shipping container where fugitive gunman Dezi Freeman hid after shooting dead two police officers.

image.altText: Items inside a shipping container.

items.title: Controversial rapper's bodyguard allegedly assaulted in Sydney abduction

children.content: Five people have been arrested after police rescued a controversial rapper's bodyguard who was allegedly kidnapped from his Guildford home.

image.altText: Forensic detectives remove evidence from an abduction scene.

items.title: More women like Celine are being diagnosed with ADHD. We're not sure why

children.content: Within the sharp increase of ADHD rates in Australia lies a hidden story — more women are being prescribed medication than men. Understanding why is more complicated.

articles.title: Think you have ADHD? Here's what getting assessed looks like

children.content: Who can diagnose ADHD, what a proper assessment should include, and the red flags to look out for.

image.altText: A close up of a person marking a page on a clipboard, sitting across from a person clasping their hands.

articles.title: The neighbourhoods where ADHD rates are skyrocketing

children.content: ADHD diagnoses in adults have exploded across Australia since 2017. For the first time, we have the data that shows where it is — and isn't — happening.

image.altText: A search bar saying "Enter your area" is superimposed on a map of Australia against a blue-tinged backdrop of medication.

children.content: The federal government is funnelling $7 billion into technology that can defeat other drones. Follow live.

items.title: NRL overhauls training rules in landmark player safety move

children.content: The move to restrict training loads is aimed at mitigating injuries, including player head injuries, which continue to be a major issue for all contact sports.

image.altText: a rugby league coach at training

items.title: Living in an apartment with your dog? Here are four things to consider

children.content: Providing opportunities for your dog to explore, giving them a safe and comfortable space to retreat to — as well as a good routine — are key to keeping your dog happy.

children.content: The next Formula 1 race in Miami will be a testing and proving ground for a number of new rules aimed at improving performance and safety.

items.title: US officials press Cuba for reforms in 'respectful' meeting in Havana

children.content: Cuba says the exchange was conducted "respectfully and professionally" and that the US delegation did not issue any threats or deadlines.

children.content: The king's moving homage comes as the final master plan for the memorial for the nation's longest-serving monarch is unveiled.

children.content: Winds of more than 100 kilometres an hour swept along WA's southern coast last night, with serious damage reported in Bunbury and Esperance.

items.title: One dead and six injured after shooting at Mexico's Teotihuacán pyramids

children.content: A Canadian tourist has died and at least six other people have been injured after a man opened fire while atop of one of the historic Teotihuacán pyramids in Mexico.

image.altText: Several people walking on a pyramid.

items.title: SA childcare centre forced to close over seven supervision incidents

children.content: Another Adelaide childcare centre has been forced to shut its doors for three months after seven incidents of children being left unsupervised.

image.altText: Edge Early Learning Centre Munno Para West

items.title: Japan warns of increased risk of mega-quake after 7.7-magnitude one

children.content: An earthquake with a preliminary magnitude of 7.7 struck off the north-eastern coast of Japan, triggering tsunami warnings and widespread evacuation orders affecting about 170,000 people.

image.altText: NHK news report of Japan earthquake and tsunami on April 20th, 2026

items.title: For the first time in decades, cash use in Australia has increased

children.content: A new report has found cash use has dropped by more than 50 per cent in almost 20 years. But there has been a slight increase in cash payments since 2022.

image.altText: Australian dollar notes.

items.title: US singer D4vd pleads not guilty to murdering 14yo girl found in his Tesla

children.content: The 21-year-old, who rose to fame after going viral on TikTok, has pleaded not guilty to a murder charge of a 14-year-old whose dismembered body was found in his seemingly abandoned Tesla.

image.altText: A man sits on a couch wearing a dark bandana, vlack shorts, a yellow mesh top and sunglasses.

items.title: FBI director sues The Atlantic for $348m over report detailing misconduct

children.content: Kash Patel denies all allegations in the report, which quotes numerous sources alleging drinking and absences.

image.altText: A man wearing a cap and jacket emblazoned with the FBI's logo

items.title: UK PM shifts blame to Foreign Office in bid to contain Mandelson scandal

children.content: The crisis around the embattled British prime minister continues to grow, with Sir Keir Starmer facing questions from MPs about what he knew of the vetting process of Peter Mandelson.

image.altText: Sir Keir Starmer walking out of Downing Street holding a folder.

items.title: 10,000 US troops join Australians for Philippines training exercise

children.content: Troops from the US, Philippines, Australia and Japan unite for the largest military exercise in "real-world conditions" to ensure security in the Indo-Pacific region. But China has criticised the drill.

image.altText: Two men in military uniform raises their fists.

items.title: 'It's just bulldozed through': Native title holders allege cultural sites have been destroyed by mining company

children.content: A First Nations group on South Australia's Yorke Peninsula says a mining company has bulldozed areas of cultural significance and has started development without a formal agreement with Traditional Owners, but the company says it has "continuously engaged" with the group.

gallery.altText: Two Aboriginal men in black shirts standing alongside each other with a serious expression to the camera

thumbnail.altText: Two Aboriginal men in black shirts standing alongside each other with a serious expression to the camera

items.title: E-bike rider dies after colliding with public bus in Perth's north-east

children.content: A 50-year-old man dies at the scene after his e-bike is involved in a crash with a Transperth bus at an intersection in Swan View, with police appealing for witnesses.

image.altText: Blue and white tape across a road

items.title: 'Large crocodile' attacks man in WA's far north

children.content: A man in his 20s has been flown to a Perth hospital after he was attacked by a crocodile at a popular camping spot north of Broome.

image.altText: A remote traffic sign

items.title: Turtles buried under mud at NSW wetlands after water inflows halted

children.content: Researchers in north-west NSW are wading through thick mud in an attempt to save hundreds of turtles after the NSW government decided to stop supplying water to the protected wetland.
Conservation biologists say the Gingham Waterhole in the Gwydir Wetlands near Moree is facing an "ecological disaster".

image.altText: Two people pull a turtle out of mud and bag it up

gallery.altText: Image shows turtles in container covered in mud.

thumbnail.altText: Image shows turtles in container covered in mud.
